fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 47-year-old Asian American female with chronic psychotic disorder with moderate symptoms.
fields.Subjective: Ms. Lin attended appointment with husband who assists with treatment coordination. Husband confirms she takes olanzapine 15 mg nightly via his supervision, no missed doses. Despite reliable adherence, continues experiencing moderate symptom burden. Reports daily auditory hallucinations in Mandarin of voices criticizing her parenting and homemaking. Also endorses paranoid belief that neighbors judge her family negatively, causing social avoidance. Sleep disrupted at 5-6 hours with middle insomnia. Appetite variable, weight stable at 158 lbs. Not working outside home, cares for two teenage children. Limited activities outside home due to anxiety and paranoia. Husband reports she has good days and difficult days. Mood 'worried' rated 4/10. Denies substance use. Safety: No SI/HI. (Assessed by Dr. Jennifer Huang, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Middle-aged female, adequate grooming, appears anxious
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Auditory hallucinations in native language, paranoid ideation about neighbors

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: Glucose 114 mg/dL; lipids mildly elevated; CBC normal.

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Other chronic psychotic disorder with persistent symptoms and anxiety.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Married; stay-at-home mother of two; limited English; culturally isolated; husband provides support.
Assessment_and_Plan: Will increase olanzapine to 20 mg qHS.
Referred to Asian community mental health program.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Jennifer Huang, MD, Psychiatrist — Asian American Mental Wellness, 3421 Chinatown Plaza, San Francisco, CA 94108, Phone: (555) 661-3487, Fax: (555) 661-3488
